exemplar.title: M25 #1: Do historians and human scientists have an ethical obligation to follow the directive: "do not ignore contradictory evidence"? Discuss with reference to history and the human sciences.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #1: Do historians and human scientists have an ethical obligation to follow the directive: "do not ignore contradictory evidence"? Discuss with reference to history and the human sciences.

strengths: Clear and sustained focus on the prescribed title throughout, anchored by an explicitly formulated central knowledge question.
Arguments are generally coherent and supported by well-integrated, discipline-specific examples (e.g., the 2008 financial crisis, Hardin vs. Ostrom, the Vinland Map).
Effective linkage of examples to areas of knowledge rather than presenting them as stand-alone anecdotes.
Awareness of alternative viewpoints is evident, with a balanced consideration of contradictory evidence in history and the human sciences.
weaknesses: Analysis does not consistently probe underlying TOK concepts (methods, bias, perspectives) with sufficient depth or precision.
Key terminology (âauthenticity,â âethical obligationâ) is applied loosely and would benefit from more rigorous definitions.
Several arguments end abruptly or conflate separate issues (e.g., methodological scope vs. ethical obligation), indicating uneven structural development.
Conclusion restates earlier points without synthesizing them into a definitive, well-justified stance on the title.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay clearly meets the descriptors for the 7â8 band: it sustains focus on the title, frames and revisits a central knowledge question, and uses a range of pertinent examples that are effectively linked to the areas of knowledge. However, it does not consistently exhibit the depth of critical examination, precise use of TOK terminology, or the fully developed evaluative moves required for the top band (9â10). Structural lapses (abrupt paragraph endings, an underdeveloped conclusion) and only uneven exploration of perspectives further justify placing it firmly at band 7. Consequently, a score of 7/10 accurately reflects the balance of strengths and areas for improvement.

annotations.comment: The essay begins with a precise restatement of the prescribed title, demonstrating clear engagement and ensuring the discussion is directly grounded in the TOK question.
Consider adding clearer signposting between the exploration of history and human sciences to guide the reader through the progression of arguments across areas of knowledge.
The discussion of the 2008 financial crisis effectively integrates a real-world example to illustrate how ignoring contradictory data in the human sciences has tangible consequences.
The formulation and explicit articulation of the central knowledge question provides a strong focus for the essay, helping to frame the subsequent analysis around the prescribed title.

The definition of âcontradictory evidenceâ is broad and would benefit from more precise criteria or TOK terminology to clarify what qualifies as valid contradictory evidence in each area of knowledge.
The terms âauthenticityâ and âethical obligationâ are applied loosely; define these key TOK concepts more rigorously to enhance analytical precision.
The paragraph ends abruptly at âThe basis of Hardinâs theoryâ without completing the thought; ensure each argument is fully developed and clearly concluded.

The claim that Roubiniâs warnings were dismissed as pessimism could be strengthened by examining how professional biases and peer reception influence the acknowledgment of contradictory perspectives.
The argument conflates methodological scope with ethical obligation; consider distinguishing when theoretical generalization justifies selective evidence without undermining professional ethics.
The Vinland Map case effectively demonstrates the tension between initial contradictory findings and the subsequent authentication processes in history.
The concluding discussion restates earlier points but fails to synthesize the arguments into a coherent final evaluation of the title.
The bibliography lists the same source twice; ensure each reference appears only once to maintain clarity and avoid redundancy.
The essay asserts that contradictory evidence was ignored due to power dynamics but stops short of analyzing the ethical or methodological factors that led economists to discount dissenting data.
The suggestion that Hardin should have used narrower universalist language conflates methodological choices with ethical duties; more TOK analysis of professional norms is needed.
The use of Hardinâs âTragedy of the Commonsâ provides a compelling counterexample, showing when overlooking contradictory evidence might advance a theoryâs explanatory power.
Introducing Elinor Ostromâs research offers a clear historical counterpoint to Hardin, effectively demonstrating how contradictory evidence can reshape theories in the human sciences.
The assertion about contemporary peer-reviewed conventions is stated without evidence; provide concrete examples of how publication norms govern inclusion or exclusion of contradictory data.
The example of Barbara Tuchmanâs The Guns of August illustrates how a historianâs ethical commitment to evidence can yield a richer, more balanced account of World War I origins.
While Tuchmanâs integration of evidence is noted, the essay does not analyze her source-criticism methods or how she weighed conflicting accounts, which is central to historiographical ethics.
Link this case to the TOK concept of corroboration by discussing how multiple independent lines of evidence strengthen or undermine a historical claim.
The statement on the benefits of investigating contradictory evidence is broad; support it with specific TOK terminology such as bias reduction or perspective expansion.
The treatment of unverified evidence as a binary choice to accept or reject is oversimplified; consider discussing how historians assess credibility through corroboration and context.
The essay mentions the mapâs debunking but neglects to explain the scientific or historiographic methods used to test its authenticity, limiting the depth of analysis.
The essay stops short of delivering a clear, well-justified stance on the prescribed title; a definitive conclusion would strengthen the overall argument.

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
